content: If we map Merriam-Webster’s core definition of an agent as "any entity that acts to produce an effect" to the 2025 development of ICM-powered autonomous agents, we can frame biological pathogens (the classic biological agents defined on Wikipedia) as naturally occurring ICM-driven curiosity agents that evolve by exploring uncharted host environment niches for intrinsic reward. This re-framing suggests that existing ICM design for swarm UAVs can be adapted to model early zoonotic spillover events more accurately than traditional epidemiological models.
